subject: DevOps
title: Runtime Container Security
description: Learn Runtime Container Security in one minute.

hook.text: A root container was compromised. The attacker escaped to the host and reached all other containers.

buildup.text: Containers share the host kernel. A container escape grants access to everything on that machine.

discovery.text: Never run as root. Use read-only filesystems. Drop unneeded Linux capabilities. Limit resources.

twist.text: Seccomp and AppArmor restrict system calls a container can make. Even if breached, it can't do much.

climax.text: Falco detects suspicious runtime behavior: unexpected shells, network connections, and file access.

punchline.text: Don't just scan images. Watch what containers do at runtime.

quiz.question: Why should containers not run as the root user?
options: A compromised root container can escape and access the host system
Root is slower than non-root users
